modules.module_title: Introduction to Telecalling, Voice Modulation & Tone
Inbound & Outbound Calling Strategies, Scripts & Openings
Telephone Etiquette, Active Listening & Call Control
Vocabulary, Phrasal Verbs & Common Expressions on Call
Professional Ethics, confidentiality & Telecalling CRM Tools

questions.question: Phone Etiquette & Telecalling Skills ka main focus kya hota hai?

options.A: Telephone calls ke through professional communication, customer relationship building aur sales conversion achieve karna
options.B: Only developing database program codes on servers
options.C: Repairing physical telephone cable wires in street areas

questions.explanation: Telecalling skills caller ki voice clarity, polite scripting, handling rejections aur call centers operations ko manage karti hain.

questions.question: Inbound Calling vs Outbound Calling systems checks me Outbound calling kya hoti hai?

options.A: Agent ka customer/client ko call perform karna (eg sales, follow-ups, telemarketing)
options.B: Customer ka help desk department me query call initiate karna
options.C: Internal office intercom calls between managers

questions.explanation: Outbound calls company team active parameters par launch karti hai leads connect karne ke liye, jabki customer call inbound me count hoti hai.

questions.question: Telephone communication me caller ke clear pronunciation checks coordinate 'Smile on Voice' kya represent karta hai?

options.A: Smile karte hue bolna (awaaz me natural warmth aur politeness generate hoti hai jo listener feel karta hai)
options.B: Laughing loudly during customer talks
options.C: Making funny facial expressions at screen

questions.explanation: Smile on voice customer comfort aur agent friendliness establish karti hai, raising conversation success margins.

questions.question: Telephone greetings standard rules me, if client is kept on hold, how often should agent talk to client?

options.A: Retrieve line every 30-45 seconds to assure client: 'Thank you for waiting, I am still processing details'
options.B: Keep line silent for 30 minutes without talking
options.C: Disconnect line silently to save company money

questions.explanation: Regular checkbacks customer patience level secure karte hain, avoiding high abandonment loops.

questions.question: Outbound sales calling me 'Handling Objections' steps me what is the standard professional approach?

options.A: Listen to customer concern fully, validate their hesitation politely, and offer customized solution value
options.B: Argue that customer is wrong and push sales force fully
options.C: Disconnect call immediately if customer says 'not interested'

questions.explanation: Objections queries satisfy karna transaction conversions ko lead karta hai. understanding customer issues is key factor.

questions.question: Professional call centers me 'AHT' (Average Handling Time) checks parameter me kya components calculate hote hain?

options.A: Total talk time + Hold time + After-call wrap work duration (eg updating CRM entries)
options.B: Total hour counts of agent break margins
options.C: Time taken to dial client phone digits

questions.explanation: AHT is standard operational metric checking agent efficiency and processing speeds.

questions.question: Outbound telemarketing checks me 'DND Registry' kya signify karti hai?

options.A: Do Not Disturb list (DND registered numbers par legal regulations ke under sales calling ban hoti hai)
options.B: Daily Network Delivery records
options.C: Department of National Database

questions.explanation: DND list compliance telecom regulatory laws checks (eg TRAI) me absolute mandatory rules me count hota hai.

questions.question: Telecaller call endings polite guidelines checks me correct closure greeting sentence?

options.A: 'Thank you for calling [Company Name], have a great day ahead.'
options.B: 'Yes, bye bye disconnect now.'
options.C: 'I have solved it, no need to call again.'

questions.explanation: Polite endings validate service quality, leaving a neat, positive brand impression.

questions.question: Customer databases updates checks me, telecallers CRM screen entries kab update karte hain?

options.A: Real-time during call conversation or immediately during After-Call Work (ACW) wrap phase
options.B: Once a week at end of shift logs
options.C: Only if customer orders expensive items

questions.explanation: Immediate CRM entries keep databases accurate, helping next department route tickets efficiently.

questions.question: Corporate ethics logs checks me caller information sharing policy limits?

options.A: Never share customer phone directories or call recordings with unauthorized third parties
options.B: Post caller name list on local public websites for publicity
options.C: Sell numbers list to third-party telemarketing firms secretly

questions.explanation: Protecting user data builds customer trust and fulfills local privacy regulations (like GDPR or TRAI guidelines).

questions.question: Inbound calling centers 'Interactive Voice Response' what is standard abbreviation?

options.A: IVR (system that routes callers to specific departments via press key inputs)
options.B: Internet Voice Recorder
options.C: Internal Vocal Register

questions.explanation: IVR systems route incoming traffic efficiently (eg 'Press 1 for Sales, Press 2 for Support'), reducing agent queue waits.

questions.question: Voice modulation checks 'Pitch vs Volume' what parameter shows calmness on call?

options.A: Low-to-medium pitch with clear vocal projection (monotone shouting increases customer panic)
options.B: Loud screaming volume to dominate caller
options.C: Whispering so caller can barely hear you

questions.explanation: A calm, well-modulated pitch projects stability, helping defuse customer anxiety during support runs.

questions.question: Outbound sales calling 'Gatekeeper handling' who is a gatekeeper?

options.A: Receptionist or secretary who screens calls before they reach the decision maker
options.B: Security guard at corporate parking gate
options.C: Software firewall program on servers

questions.explanation: Navigating the gatekeeper politely is key. State your purpose clearly without sounding like a cold sales bot.

questions.question: Telecalling metrics 'First Call Resolution' (FCR) why is it the gold standard in inbound centers?

options.A: Saves operational costs and raises customer satisfaction CSAT by avoiding repeat query calls
options.B: Allows agents to take longer tea breaks
options.C: Limits call durations to exactly 1 minute

questions.explanation: FCR measures how often issues are resolved on the first call, eliminating follow-up calls.

questions.question: Telecaller operations 'Shift scheduling' why are call schedules adjusted to client time zones?

options.A: Avoids calling clients at late night or early morning hours, respecting privacy norms
options.B: Reduces call routing taxes
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms

questions.explanation: Calling clients at inappropriate hours breaches ethics codes. Respecting timezone limits keeps response rates high.

questions.question: Time zones respect telecalling schedule?

options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms

questions.question: Outbound calling 'Opening hook statement' why are initial 10 seconds critical?

options.A: Audience decides to stay on call or hang up within first 10 seconds of introduction
options.B: It calculates call connection costs on server
options.C: The caller checks spelling words

questions.explanation: First impressions are made quickly. A polite introduction and clear value hook prevent early hang-ups.

questions.question: Inbound calling centers 'Abandonment rate metrics' what does it measure?

options.A: Percentage of incoming calls disconnected by callers before connecting with an agent
options.B: Percentage of staff absent from rosters
options.C: Total number of outbound sales target calls missed

questions.explanation: High abandonment rates indicate long wait times in queues, alerting managers to adjust staffing.

questions.question: Telephone communication 'Phonics spelling codes' why are standard words (Alpha, Bravo) used on call?

options.A: Prevents spelling errors for similar sounding letters (eg 'B' vs 'D' or 'M' vs 'N') over bad phone lines
options.B: It is a secret security translation code
options.C: Required by international call taxes

questions.explanation: Using standard phonics (eg 'A for Alpha, B for Bravo') ensures names and emails are recorded accurately in CRM databases.

questions.question: Handling difficult callers 'Broken record technique' when is it used?

options.A: Repeating the same polite, firm message in the same calm tone if customer keeps repeating demands
options.B: Playing recorded music on hold loops continuously
options.C: Disconnecting line and blaming software error

questions.explanation: This technique helps maintain boundaries without getting into circular arguments with emotional callers.

questions.question: Telecaller operations 'DND list verification processes' what happens before outbound campaigns start?

options.A: Scrub call list against national DND registries to filter out registered numbers
options.B: Ignore numbers list and dial all directly
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms

questions.explanation: Scrubbing numbers against the DND database prevents regulatory fines, keeping campaigns compliant.

questions.question: First 10 seconds hang up hook?

options.A: Audience decides to stay on call or hang up within first 10 seconds of introduction
options.B: It calculates call connection costs on server
options.C: The caller checks spelling words

questions.explanation: First impressions are made quickly. A polite introduction and clear value hook prevent early hang-ups.

options.A: Percentage of incoming calls disconnected by callers before connecting with an agent
options.B: Percentage of staff absent from rosters
options.C: Total number of outbound sales target calls missed

questions.explanation: High abandonment rates indicate long wait times in queues, alerting managers to adjust staffing.

questions.explanation: Using standard phonics (eg 'A for Alpha, B for Bravo') ensures names and emails are recorded accurately in CRM databases.

options.B: Playing recorded music on hold loops continuously
options.C: Disconnecting line and blaming software error

questions.explanation: This technique helps maintain boundaries without getting into circular arguments with emotional callers.

questions.question: Scrub call list DND registry campaign?

options.A: Scrub call list against national DND registries to filter out registered numbers
options.B: Ignore numbers list and dial all directly
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms

questions.explanation: Scrubbing numbers against the DND database prevents regulatory fines, keeping campaigns compliant.

questions.question: Inbound call support 'Verification protocols' what details are confirmed before sharing account info?

options.A: Full name, registered email, and transaction/order ID (never share details without verification)
options.B: User's childhood address and hobbies
options.C: Card PIN numbers and passwords directly

questions.explanation: Running security checks protects user accounts from social engineering and data leaks.

questions.question: Voice health 'Vocal fatigue prevention' how do call agents protect their vocal cords during shifts?

options.A: Drink warm water regularly, avoid clearing throat aggressively, and use correct diaphragm projection
options.B: Drink iced soft drinks continually
options.C: Shout loudly to ensure mic records clearly
options.D: Vocal care is not needed for calling

questions.explanation: Warm water lubricates vocal folds, preventing strain during long telecalling shifts.

questions.question: Telephone communication 'Handling silence on call' how should agent respond if processing takes 15 seconds?

options.A: Inform customer: 'I am looking up the details, it will take another 10 seconds. Thank you for waiting.'
options.B: Leave the line completely silent without updates
options.C: Hum songs or talk with adjacent desk agents
options.D: Disconnect call if processing takes time

questions.explanation: Informing the caller prevents them from thinking they were disconnected, maintaining professional phone manners.

questions.question: Telecalling operations 'Cold calling vs Warm calling' what defines Warm calling?

options.A: Contacting a lead who has previously expressed interest, visited website, or registered details
options.B: Dialing random numbers from database directories
options.C: Calling clients in warm summer climates

questions.explanation: Warm calling targets leads who are already familiar with the brand, resulting in higher conversion rates.

questions.question: CRM systems 'Call disposition codes' what are they?

options.A: Standard outcome codes (eg 'Call Answered', 'Busy', 'Follow-up Scheduled', 'No Answer') selected after every call
options.B: Secret translation codes for database security
options.C: Codes used to calculate agent commission payouts
options.D: No outcomes are recorded in CRM

questions.explanation: Call disposition codes categorize outcomes, helping teams manage follow-ups and monitor campaign metrics.

questions.question: Inbound verification name email ID?

options.B: User's childhood address and hobbies
options.C: Card PIN numbers and passwords directly

questions.explanation: Running security checks protects user accounts from social engineering and data leaks.

options.B: Secret translation codes for database security
options.C: Codes used to calculate agent commission payouts
options.D: No outcomes are recorded in CRM

questions.explanation: Call disposition codes categorize outcomes, helping teams manage follow-ups and monitor campaign metrics.

questions.question: Telephone etiquette 'Placing call on hold' what is incorrect hold behavior?

options.A: Placing customer on hold silently without asking permission or stating reason
options.B: Asking client permission before hold
options.C: Updating caller every 45 seconds
options.D: Holding is a standard call procedure

questions.explanation: Placing a call on hold silently is considered rude, leading to customer frustration and early hang-ups.

questions.question: Outbound sales calling 'The Script usage' what is standard guideline for scripts?

options.A: Use script as guide to cover key points, but speak naturally and adapt to customer responses (avoid robotic reading)
options.B: Read script word-for-word at high speed
options.C: Ignore script completely and guess product features
options.D: No scripts are used in professional sales

questions.explanation: Robotic script reading drives prospects away. Speaking naturally helps build rapport and trust.

questions.question: Telecaller operations 'DND scrub records' how often are marketing databases updated?

options.A: Regularly before starting any outbound marketing campaigns (usually every 15-30 days)
options.B: Once a year only
options.C: No scrub updates are required after setup

questions.explanation: DND registrations change over time. Regular database scrubbing keeps marketing lists compliant with telecom laws.

questions.question: Telephone communication 'Voice quality indicators' what does 'Muffled voice' on call indicate?

options.A: Microphone is kept too close to mouth, blockages in mic ports, or poor hardware settings
options.B: Agent is speaking too loud
options.C: Call connection has high latency

questions.explanation: Muffled sound happens when mic positioning or port placement is off. Keep mic 2 inches away from lips.

questions.question: Telecalling business logistics 'Daily call target quotas' why are quotas tracked?

options.A: Monitors campaign outreach efficiency, agent productivity rates, and sales pipeline growth
options.B: Calculates local office property taxes
options.C: Required by Google AdSense checks

questions.explanation: Tracking call metrics (eg dials, talk time) helps managers monitor agent productivity and optimize outreach.

questions.question: Silent hold without permission incorrect?

options.A: Placing customer on hold silently without asking permission or stating reason
options.B: Asking client permission before hold
options.C: Updating caller every 45 seconds
options.D: Holding is a standard call procedure

questions.explanation: Placing a call on hold silently is considered rude, leading to customer frustration and early hang-ups.

options.C: Required by Google AdSense checks

questions.explanation: Tracking call metrics (eg dials, talk time) helps managers monitor agent productivity and optimize outreach.

questions.question: Inbound call centers 'Handling call routing overflows' what is standard backup solution?

options.A: Routing calls to backup voicemail boxes or callback request system queues
options.B: Disconnecting incoming calls automatically
options.C: Rerouting calls to random employees personal mobiles

questions.explanation: Callback systems keep customers from hanging up in frustration, protecting service quality metrics.

questions.question: Telecalling vocabulary 'Cold Lead' what does it indicate?

options.A: A contact who has zero prior knowledge about your company or product offerings
options.B: A lead who resides in cold geographic climates
options.C: A customer who has completed multiple purchases

questions.explanation: Cold leads require a clear value pitch to build trust and capture interest, unlike warm leads.

questions.question: Telephone conversations 'Phrasal Verbs' what does 'hang up' mean?

options.A: End the telephone connection (keep receiver down)
options.B: Put call on hold loops
options.C: Transfer call to manager desk

questions.explanation: 'Hang up' is standard phrasal verb meaning terminating the active telephone session.

questions.question: Handling difficult callers 'The Empathy statement' what phrase is most effective?

options.A: 'I understand how frustrating this delay must be, and I will resolve this for you.'
options.B: 'It is not our department's fault, wait quietly.'
options.C: 'Calm down, screaming does not help.'
options.D: No empathy statements are used on call

questions.explanation: Validating client frustration defuses anger, helping transition the call to a cooperative resolution.

questions.question: Telecaller operations CRM systems 'Lead disposition updates' why are they mandatory?

options.A: Prevents redundant calls to same client, tracking progress status of campaigns
options.B: Required by regional telecom taxes
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms

questions.explanation: Logging dispositions (eg 'do not call', 'scheduled callback') prevents redundant dials, respecting customer boundaries.

questions.question: Call overflow voicemail backup callback?

options.A: Routing calls to backup voicemail boxes or callback request system queues
options.B: Disconnecting incoming calls automatically
options.C: Rerouting calls to random employees personal mobiles

questions.explanation: Callback systems keep customers from hanging up in frustration, protecting service quality metrics.

questions.explanation: Cold leads require a clear value pitch to build trust and capture interest, unlike warm leads.

questions.question: Inbound call support 'Verification security' why are security questions asked?

options.A: Protects customer account data, preventing social engineering attacks by unauthorized callers
options.B: Adds extra billing minutes to calls
options.C: Checks caller's academic history
options.D: Verification is not required by law

questions.explanation: Asking standard security questions prevents data leaks, protecting client accounts from unauthorized access.

questions.question: Vocal techniques 'Tone projection' why are agents advised to sit upright?

options.A: Improves lung capacity and diaphragm breathing, resulting in a clearer, more confident voice
options.B: UPright sitting reduces typing speed
options.C: Required by building fire codes
options.D: Posture has no impact on voice tone

questions.explanation: Sitting upright supports your diaphragm, helping you project a clear, confident voice on calls.

questions.question: Call center terminology 'Dead Air' what does it mean?

options.A: Periods of complete silence on the call where neither the agent nor the customer is speaking
options.B: Static sound noise on bad lines
options.C: Call disconnects due to power failures

questions.explanation: Dead Air sounds unprofessional and confuses callers. Keep the customer updated while looking up their files.

questions.question: Handling rejections 'The No-go customer response' how should sales agent react if rejected?

options.A: Thank the customer politely for their time, log outcome in CRM, and move to next call (avoid getting angry)
options.B: Argue with the customer to force change of mind
options.C: Hang up the call immediately mid-sentence
options.D: Call back from different numbers to bypass blocks

questions.explanation: Handling rejections politely preserves brand reputation, keeping the door open for future follow-up campaigns.

questions.question: Telecaller campaign operations 'Lead lists sorting' what is standard practice?

options.A: Sort leads by category (eg cold, warm, hot) to target the most promising prospects first
options.B: Dial leads in random unsorted order daily
options.C: Ignore lead categories completely

questions.explanation: Sorting leads helps agents prioritize their outreach, maximizing campaign efficiency.

questions.question: Inbound verification security questions?

options.A: Protects customer account data, preventing social engineering attacks by unauthorized callers
options.B: Adds extra billing minutes to calls
options.C: Checks caller's academic history
options.D: Verification is not required by law

questions.explanation: Asking standard security questions prevents data leaks, protecting client accounts from unauthorized access.

questions.question: Posture upright diaphragm vocal projection?

options.A: Improves lung capacity and diaphragm breathing, resulting in a clearer, more confident voice
options.B: UPright sitting reduces typing speed
options.C: Required by building fire codes
options.D: Posture has no impact on voice tone

questions.explanation: Sitting upright supports your diaphragm, helping you project a clear, confident voice on calls.

questions.explanation: Handling rejections politely preserves brand reputation, keeping the door open for future follow-up campaigns.

options.B: Dial leads in random unsorted order daily
options.C: Ignore lead categories completely

questions.explanation: Sorting leads helps agents prioritize their outreach, maximizing campaign efficiency.

questions.question: Telephone etiquette 'Placing calls on hold' what is maximum recommended hold time?

options.A: Max 1 to 2 minutes (request permission and check back regularly)
options.B: 10 minutes silent hold is standard
options.C: Hold times have no limits
options.D: Holding is not used in business calls

questions.explanation: Long hold times frustrate callers. Regular updates help keep the customer engaged.

questions.question: Outbound telemarketing 'The Hook statement' when is it spoken in the script?

options.A: Immediately after greeting and introducing yourself (states the benefit of the call)
options.B: At the end of the call during sign-off
options.C: Only if the customer requests details
options.D: Hooks are not used in script openings

questions.explanation: The hook highlights the value proposition early, keeping the customer on the line.

questions.question: Call center metrics 'FCR tracking' what does it indicate about agent performance?

options.A: High problem-solving skills and thoroughness (resolving issues without transfers)
options.B: Fast typing speeds on CRM screens
options.C: Short talk times on call queues
options.D: FCR has zero impact on performance evaluation

questions.explanation: FCR measures how often issues are resolved on the first call, highlighting agent capability.

questions.question: Handling difficult callers 'Setting limits politely' what phrase is best?

options.A: 'I am eager to assist you, but I must ask you to use respectful language so I can do so.'
options.B: 'If you scream again, I will hang up.'
options.C: 'I am transferring you to legal department.'

questions.explanation: Setting firm, polite boundaries helps manage aggressive callers without escalating tension.

questions.question: Telecaller operations 'DND scrub records' what database list is referenced?

options.A: National Customer Preference Register (NCPR - DND database list)
options.B: Daily sales ledger registries
options.C: Company staff roster lists

questions.explanation: Scrubbing outbound lists against the NCPR database prevents compliance violations and regulatory fines.

questions.question: Maximum recommended hold time 2 minutes?

options.A: Max 1 to 2 minutes (request permission and check back regularly)
options.B: 10 minutes silent hold is standard
options.C: Hold times have no limits
options.D: Holding is not used in business calls

questions.explanation: Long hold times frustrate callers. Regular updates help keep the customer engaged.

questions.question: Outbound hook benefits statement?

options.A: Immediately after greeting and introducing yourself (states the benefit of the call)
options.B: At the end of the call during sign-off
options.C: Only if the customer requests details
options.D: Hooks are not used in script openings

questions.explanation: The hook highlights the value proposition early, keeping the customer on the line.

questions.question: FCR high problem solving skills?

options.A: High problem-solving skills and thoroughness (resolving issues without transfers)
options.B: Fast typing speeds on CRM screens
options.C: Short talk times on call queues
options.D: FCR has zero impact on performance evaluation

questions.explanation: FCR measures how often issues are resolved on the first call, highlighting agent capability.

questions.question: Difficult caller limit setting respectful?

options.A: 'I am eager to assist you, but I must ask you to use respectful language so I can do so.'
options.B: 'If you scream again, I will hang up.'
options.C: 'I am transferring you to legal department.'

questions.explanation: Setting firm, polite boundaries helps manage aggressive callers without escalating tension.

questions.question: NCPR DND scrub database registry?

options.A: National Customer Preference Register (NCPR - DND database list)
options.B: Daily sales ledger registries
options.C: Company staff roster lists

questions.explanation: Scrubbing outbound lists against the NCPR database prevents compliance violations and regulatory fines.

questions.question: Phone Etiquette & Telecalling Skills final launch audits why voice recording checks are key final step?

options.A: Verifies correct phonetic pronunciations, caller response pacing, and smile on voice tone meets standards
options.B: Video editing file sizes compression
options.C: Subtitles translate tools automatic

questions.explanation: Auditing sample recordings ensures callers maintain professional tone and meet communication standards.

questions.question: Phone communication skills core focus?

options.A: Telephone calls ke through professional communication, customer relationship building aur sales conversion achieve karna
options.B: Only developing database program codes on servers
options.C: Repairing physical telephone cable wires in street areas

questions.explanation: Telecalling skills caller ki voice clarity, polite scripting, handling rejections aur call centers operations ko manage karti hain.

questions.question: Outbound telemarketing client call definition?

options.A: Agent ka customer/client ko call perform karna (eg sales, follow-ups, telemarketing)
options.B: Customer ka help desk department me query call initiate karna
options.C: Internal office intercom calls between managers

questions.explanation: Outbound calls company team active parameters par launch karti hai leads connect karne ke liye, jabki customer call inbound me count hoti hai.

questions.question: Voice modulation smile natural warmth?

options.A: Smile karte hue bolna (awaaz me natural warmth aur politeness generate hoti hai jo listener feel karta hai)
options.B: Laughing loudly during customer talks
options.C: Making funny facial expressions at screen

questions.explanation: Smile on voice customer comfort aur agent friendliness establish karti hai, raising conversation success margins.

questions.question: Customer hold checks 30 45 seconds?

options.A: Retrieve line every 30-45 seconds to assure client: 'Thank you for waiting, I am still processing details'
options.B: Keep line silent for 30 minutes without talking
options.C: Disconnect line silently to save company money

questions.explanation: Regular checkbacks customer patience level secure karte hain, avoiding high abandonment loops.

questions.question: Objection handling customer concerns resolution?

options.A: Listen to customer concern fully, validate their hesitation politely, and offer customized solution value
options.B: Argue that customer is wrong and push sales force fully
options.C: Disconnect call immediately if customer says 'not interested'

questions.explanation: Objections queries satisfy karna transaction conversions ko lead karta hai. understanding customer issues is key factor.

questions.question: AHT call center wrap time calculations?

options.A: Total talk time + Hold time + After-call wrap work duration (eg updating CRM entries)
options.B: Total hour counts of agent break margins
options.C: Time taken to dial client phone digits

questions.explanation: AHT is standard operational metric checking agent efficiency and processing speeds.

questions.question: DND list sales calling legal limits?

options.A: Do Not Disturb list (DND registered numbers par legal regulations ke under sales calling ban hoti hai)
options.B: Daily Network Delivery records
options.C: Department of National Database

questions.explanation: DND list compliance telecom regulatory laws checks (eg TRAI) me absolute mandatory rules me count hota hai.

questions.question: Polite call closure sign off greeting?

options.A: 'Thank you for calling [Company Name], have a great day ahead.'
options.B: 'Yes, bye bye disconnect now.'
options.C: 'I have solved it, no need to call again.'

questions.explanation: Polite endings validate service quality, leaving a neat, positive brand impression.

questions.question: Real time CRM update after call wrap?

options.A: Real-time during call conversation or immediately during After-Call Work (ACW) wrap phase
options.B: Once a week at end of shift logs
options.C: Only if customer orders expensive items

questions.explanation: Immediate CRM entries keep databases accurate, helping next department route tickets efficiently.
